title: pastinaca montana
intro: Pastinaca montana, also known as wild parsnip, is a biennial plant in the carrot family (Apiaceae) native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and act as a diuretic. The health benefits of wild parsnip include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of various digestive issues. Wild parsnip can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to its potential skin-irritating properties.
